message:
fix(core,codegen): recognize table-level INTEGER PRIMARY KEY as rowid alias and fix INSERT column-list handling

Table-level PRIMARY KEY(col) constraints on INTEGER columns are now
correctly detected as rowid aliases in both schema loading and compat
persistence, matching SQLite semantics for CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER,
..., PRIMARY KEY(id)).

INSERT improvements:
- Unified build_insert_target_mapping for VALUES and SELECT sources,
  replacing duplicated column-mapping logic across three code paths
- Support explicit hidden-rowid targets (INSERT INTO t(rowid, ...) ...)
  alongside IPK columns in all INSERT variants
- Duplicate target columns keep first assignment, matching SQLite behavior
- Validate target columns before execution, rejecting unknown column names
- Preserve declared type arguments (e.g. DECIMAL(10,2)) in schema metadata
  and PRAGMA table_info output
- Fill IPK column value with resolved rowid in all conflict-resolution
  branches so RETURNING and stored data stay consistent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
